alternatives.when: repeat file reads dominate a session and its SHA-256 dedup cache is worth the extra moving parts (an identical re-read collapses to a roughly 13-token reference)
you want rule-based, no-LLM distillation of command output with the raw text still retrievable on demand, plus a native MCP server

recommendations.rationale: For cutting the token cost of shell command output, rtk is the default: it is the most established of the three, holds a best verdict, and runs as a transparent proxy over common dev commands. sqz is effectively an rtk superset when repeat reads dominate, since its dedup cache turns an identical re-read into a short reference token. omni fits agents that prefer rule-based distillers with retrievable raw output and a native MCP server. All three keep their core mechanism free of any model dependency, so none adds an LLM call to the hot path.
